title: NSF Award: CAREER: Foundations of Machine Learning-Enhanced Branch-and-Bound Algorithms for Scalable Global Optimization
institution: Virginia Polytechnic Institute and State University
description: This Faculty Early Career Development Program (CAREER) grant will advance national energy security and economic welfare by developing improved tools for optimizing complex energy and industrial systems. Critical infrastructures, such as electric power grids and chemical refineries, depend on solving large optimization problems to determine safe and efficient operating conditions. NSF Award: Supporting Pathways for Advancing Readiness in Computing: AI, Mathematics, Cybersecurity and Statistics
description: This project will contribute to the national need for well-educated scientists, mathematicians, engineers, and technicians by supporting the retention and graduation of high-achieving, low-income students with demonstrated financial need at Illinois Institute of Technology. title: NSF Award: AF:Small: Quantified Polyhedral Programming
description: Any system that is required to respond to stimuli from its environment is called a reactive system. The system’s response is possibly to change its status or conditions and even affect its environment. Hence, a reactive system is an event-driven system reacting endlessly to external stimuli. It maintains an ongoing interaction with its environment, changing its responses accordingly. title: NSF Award: Optimization, Risk Management and Adaptation for Integrated Infrastructure Systems under Technological and Policy Uncertainties
institution: Regents of the University of Michigan - Ann Arbor
description: This award supports research on new models and a comprehensive framework to capture the complexities and interdependencies of infrastructure systems, while accounting for technological innovations and policy changes. Infrastructure systems, including energy, transportation, water, and telecommunication, form the backbone of modern society and are becoming increasingly complex due to emerging technologies such as renewable energy and artificial intelligence, as well as rapidly evolving public pol

title: Marie Sklodowska-Curie Postdoctoral Fellowship 2026
description: Prestigious EU-funded postdoctoral fellowship with mobility allowance and research costs. Must move to a different country. PhD required, max 8 years post-PhD.

title: ERC Starting Grant 2026
description: Up to 1.5M EUR for 5 years. For researchers 2-7 years after PhD. Any nationality, must work in EU/associated country.

title: ERC Consolidator Grant 2026
description: Up to 2M EUR for 5 years. For researchers 7-12 years after PhD.

title: NSF CAREER Award 2026
description: NSF's most prestigious award for early-career faculty. $500K+ over 5 years. Must be tenure-track at a US institution.

title: MSCA Doctoral Networks 2026
description: Fund a network of PhD positions across multiple European institutions. Typically 10-15 PhD positions per network.

title: DAAD Postdoctoral Researchers International Mobility Experience
description: Funding for postdoctoral research stays in Germany. 12-24 months.

title: NWO Veni Grant 2026
description: 250K EUR for 3 years for recently graduated PhDs to develop their research ideas at a Dutch university.

title: INFORMS Optimization Society Young Researcher Prize 2026
description: Recognizes outstanding research by a young member of INFORMS Optimization Society.

title: EURO Doctoral Dissertation Award 2026
description: Annual award for the best doctoral dissertation in OR defended in the past year.

title: INFORMS Job Board — Browse OR Positions
description: INFORMS career center with academic and industry positions in OR.

title: ORSC Job Board — OR Positions in China
description: Operations Research Society of China job listings.

title: MathJobs.org — Mathematics & OR Positions
